info.description: REST API for the MCP Data Platform. Covers admin endpoints (system, config, personas, auth keys, audit, knowledge, memory, connections), portal endpoints (assets, collections, shares, prompts, activity), and resource management.
info.title: MCP Data Platform API

get.description: Returns paginated audit events with optional filtering.
Returns unique user IDs and tool names seen in the audit log, sorted alphabetically.
Returns a single audit event by ID.
Returns audit event counts grouped by a dimension.

parameters.description: Dimension: tool_name, user_id, persona, toolkit_kind, connection
parameters.name: group_by

get.description: Returns discovery-before-query session pattern statistics.
Returns aggregate enrichment statistics including mode breakdown and token savings.
Returns aggregate audit statistics for the given time range.
Returns latency percentile statistics for the given time range.
Returns audit event counts bucketed by time resolution.

parameters.description: Time bucket resolution: minute, hour, day (default: hour)
parameters.name: resolution

get.description: Returns aggregate counts for total, successful, and failed events. Supports time and filter parameters.
Returns all API keys (key values are never exposed, only names and roles).

post.description: Generates a new API key. The key value is returned only once.

get.description: Returns the current configuration as JSON with sensitive values redacted.
Returns the current configuration as downloadable YAML. Sensitive values are redacted by default.

parameters.description: Set to 'true' to include sensitive values
parameters.name: secrets

get.description: Returns the current config store mode (file or database).
Returns all database-managed connection instances.
Returns a single database-managed connection instance by kind and name.

put.description: Creates or updates a database-managed connection instance.

get.description: Returns all toolkit connections with their tools.

get.summary: List enrichment rules for a connection

post.description: Loads the rule, applies it against the provided sample args/response/user without persisting and without invoking the live gateway, and returns the merged response + per-rule trace. Use this in the rule editor to validate bindings and merge strategy.

Generates a PKCE verifier, derives the SHA256 challenge, registers a state token, and returns the authorization URL the operator should open in their browser. The platform expects the upstream to redirect to /api/v1/admin/oauth/callback after the user authenticates.
post.summary: Begin OAuth authorization-code flow for a gateway connection

post.description: Triggers a client_credentials grant against the configured OAuth token URL, replacing the cached token. Used to recover from upstream-side credential rotations or to verify the configured client_id/client_secret without waiting for token expiry.
post.summary: Force a fresh OAuth token exchange

post.description: Re-dials a configured gateway connection using the stored config, re-discovers its tool set, and swaps the live connection atomically. Used after an upstream adds, removes, or changes tools.

get.description: Reports whether the connection is healthy, its tool count, and (when AuthMode=oauth) the current token state — expiry, last refreshed, last error.
get.summary: Get a gateway connection's runtime status

post.description: Dials the upstream described by the submitted config, lists its tools, and returns them. Does not persist anything. When the submitted config contains "[REDACTED]" sensitive fields and a row with this name already exists, the redacted fields are merged from the stored config.

get.description: Returns paginated changesets with optional filtering.
Returns a single changeset by ID.

post.description: Rolls back a changeset, restoring previous values to DataHub.

get.description: Returns paginated insights with optional filtering.
Returns aggregated insight statistics by entity, category, confidence, and status.
Returns a single insight by ID.

put.description: Update insight text, category, or confidence. Cannot edit an applied insight.
Approve or reject an insight. Status must be 'approved' or 'rejected'.

get.description: Returns paginated memory records with optional filtering.
Returns aggregated memory record statistics by dimension, category, and status.
Returns a single memory record by ID.

put.description: Update content, category, confidence, dimension, or metadata on a record.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a memory record by setting its status to archived.

get.description: Public endpoint hit by the upstream OAuth provider after the operator authenticates. Exchanges the code for tokens and stores them. Renders an HTML page on error so a stranded browser tab still gives a useful message.
Returns all configured personas with tool counts.

post.description: Creates a new persona. Only available in database config mode.

get.description: Returns a single persona with resolved tool list.

put.description: Updates an existing persona. Only available in database config mode.

delete.description: Deletes a persona. Only available in database config mode. Cannot delete the admin persona.

post.description: Evaluates the named persona's allow/deny rules against a tool name and returns the decision plus the matching pattern. Used by the admin Tools page to preview "would persona X allow this tool?" without traversing every persona.
post.summary: Preview a persona's decision for a tool

get.description: Returns platform identity, version, runtime feature availability, and config mode.
Returns all registered tools across all toolkits and platform-level tools.

post.description: Executes a tool via the MCP server and returns the result.

get.description: Returns JSON schemas for all registered tools including parameter definitions.

Returns everything the admin Tools page needs to render a single tool: kind, toolkit, connection, schema, per-persona allow/deny matrix with matched pattern, hidden state, description-override status, recent audit aggregate, and enrichment-rule count for gateway-proxied tools.

put.description: Adds or removes the named tool from the platform-wide tools.deny list. The deny list controls visibility in tools/list responses; persona auth gates execution independently. Read-modify-write through the standard config_entries store, so the value is JSON-encoded as []string and persists across restarts.

get.description: Returns activity breakdown grouped by a dimension (tool_name, user_id, persona, toolkit_kind, or connection).
Returns aggregate activity metrics for the current user within an optional time range.
Returns time-bucketed activity data for the current user.

parameters.description: Bucket resolution: minute, hour, day (default: hour)
parameters.name: resolution

get.description: Returns paginated assets owned by the current user with optional filtering.
Returns a single asset by ID. Non-owners need share access.

put.description: Updates the asset's name, description, or tags. Only the owner can update.

delete.description: Soft-deletes an asset. Only the owner can delete.

get.description: Downloads the asset's binary content from S3.

put.description: Uploads new binary content for the asset, creating a new version.

parameters.description: Change summary for the new version
parameters.name: X-Change-Summary

post.description: Creates an independent copy of a shared asset in the current user's My Assets.

get.description: Returns all shares for an asset. Only the owner can view shares.

post.description: Creates a share link or user-targeted share for an asset. Only the owner can share.

get.description: Downloads the asset's PNG thumbnail image.

put.description: Uploads a PNG thumbnail image for the asset.

get.description: Returns paginated version history for an asset.
Downloads the binary content of a specific asset version.

post.description: Reverts the asset content to a specific version by creating a new version with that content.

get.description: Returns paginated collections owned by the current user.

post.description: Creates a new collection for the current user.

get.description: Returns a single collection by ID. Non-owners need share access.

put.description: Updates a collection's name and/or description. Only the owner can update.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a collection. Only the owner can delete.

put.description: Updates the collection's display configuration. Only the owner can update.
Replaces all sections and items in a collection. Only the owner can modify.

get.description: Returns all shares for a collection. Only the owner can view shares.

post.description: Creates a share link or user-targeted share for a collection. Only the owner can share.

get.description: Downloads the collection's PNG thumbnail image.

put.description: Uploads a PNG thumbnail image for the collection. Only the owner can upload.

get.description: Returns paginated insights captured by the current user.
Returns aggregate statistics for the current user's insights.
Returns the authenticated user's profile including roles, persona, and available tools.
Returns paginated memory records for the current user with optional filtering.
Returns aggregated memory statistics grouped by dimension, category, and status.
Returns the user's personal prompts plus available global, persona, and system prompts.

post.description: Creates a new personal prompt for the current user.

put.description: Updates a personal prompt owned by the current user. Admins can update any prompt.

delete.description: Deletes a personal prompt owned by the current user. Admins can delete any prompt.

get.description: Returns paginated collections that other users have shared with the current user.
Returns paginated assets that other users have shared with the current user.

delete.description: Revokes a share by its ID. Only the asset owner can revoke.

get.description: List managed resources visible to the caller, with optional filters.

parameters.description: Filter by scope ID (persona name or user sub)
parameters.name: scope_id

post.description: Upload a new managed resource with metadata and file content.

parameters.description: Persona name or user sub (required for persona/user scopes)
parameters.name: scope_id

get.description: Retrieve metadata for a single managed resource by ID.

delete.description: Delete a managed resource, removing both the S3 blob and database metadata.

patch.description: Update mutable metadata fields of a managed resource.

get.description: Download the binary content of a managed resource.

description_overridden.description:
Description-override status (populated when a
tool.<name>.description config-entry exists).

enrichment_rule_count.description:
Number of cross-enrichment rules attached to this tool. Only
meaningful for proxied tools (kind=mcp); zero for native.

hidden_by_global_deny.description:
HiddenByGlobalDeny is true when the platform-level tools.deny
list matches this tool. GlobalDenyPattern is the matching glob.

hidden_by_persona.description:
HiddenByPersona maps persona name → true for any persona where
this tool is denied.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters.

personas.description:
Persona allow/deny matrix — one entry per database-managed
persona, with the matched pattern and source recorded.

warning.example: Store this key securely. It will not be shown again.

insight_text.example: The amount column represents gross margin before returns.

content.example: The daily_sales table in the retail schema is partitioned by date.

agent_instructions_suffix.example: Focus on schema details, data lineage, and query optimization.

description_prefix.example: This user is a data engineer responsible for ETL pipelines.

description.example: ETL pipeline development and schema management
ETL pipeline development and schema management

hidden.description:
Hidden is true when the tool is excluded from tools/list responses by
the platform-wide tools.allow / tools.deny visibility filter. Persona
authorization is independent and not reflected here.

description.example: Execute a SQL query against Trino and return results.

hidden.description: Hidden=true adds the tool to the global tools.deny list; false removes it.

authenticated_at.description:
AuthenticatedAt records when the most recent successful exchange
(initial OAuth dance or refresh) completed.

authenticated_by.description:
AuthenticatedBy is the email/id of the operator who completed the
browser flow. Empty for client_credentials and for connections that
have not yet been authorized.

needs_reauth.description:
NeedsReauth is true when the platform cannot mint an access token
without operator interaction. For authorization_code grants this
means: no stored token, or the refresh token has been revoked.
The admin UI surfaces a "Connect" button when this is true.

insight_text.example: The amount column represents gross margin before returns, not revenue.

content.example: The daily_sales table in the retail schema is partitioned by date.

description.example: A prompt for data analysis workflows

content.example: Analyze sales data for {date} grouped by region.

description.example: Generate a daily sales summary by region
Step-by-step procedures for ETL pipeline operations

scope_id.description: persona name or user sub; empty for global

tags.name: User
tags.description: Current user identity, roles, persona, and available tools.

tags.name: Activity
tags.description: Personal analytics for the authenticated user's tool usage. Timeseries, breakdowns, and summary statistics scoped to the calling user.

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: AI-generated artifacts — dashboards, reports, visualizations, and data exports. Supports HTML, JSX, SVG, Markdown, and CSV content types with versioning, thumbnails, and sharing.

tags.name: Collections
tags.description: Curated groups of assets organized into ordered sections with markdown descriptions. Collections support sharing via public links and user-level permissions.

tags.name: Knowledge
tags.description: Domain knowledge captured during AI sessions. Insights go through an admin review workflow before being written back to the data catalog. Includes insight statistics and governance lifecycle tracking.

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Persistent memory records accumulated across sessions — corrections, preferences, business context, and data quality observations. Backed by PostgreSQL with pgvector for semantic search.

tags.name: Prompts
tags.description: Reusable prompt templates with argument placeholders. Users manage personal prompts and browse available global, persona, and system prompts.

tags.name: Resources
tags.description: Human-uploaded reference materials — SQL templates, runbooks, checklists, and brand assets. Scoped by visibility (global, persona, user) and accessible to AI agents via the MCP resources protocol.

tags.name: Shares
tags.description: Asset and collection sharing via public links (token-based, time-limited) and user shares (email-based with viewer/editor permissions).

tags.name: Audit
tags.description: Platform-wide audit log of every tool call. Paginated event queries with filtering, aggregate statistics, performance percentiles, enrichment metrics, and discovery pattern analytics.

tags.name: Auth Keys
tags.description: API key management for programmatic access. Create, list, and revoke keys with role assignment and expiration. Keys from the config file are read-only.

tags.name: Config
tags.description: Platform configuration management. Read the active config, export as YAML, and manage per-key database overrides for whitelisted settings with hot-reload.

tags.name: Connections
tags.description: Toolkit connection management for Trino, DataHub, and S3 backends. View file-configured connections, create database-managed instances, and inspect connection details.

tags.name: Personas
tags.description: Role-based access control profiles that determine which tools and connections a user can access. Each persona defines allow/deny patterns, context overrides, and priority-based role mapping.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Platform identity, version, runtime feature availability, registered tools, and toolkit connections.

tags.name: Tools
tags.description: Tool schema introspection and interactive execution. Browse JSON schemas for all registered tools and execute tool calls with parameter validation.
